message:
fix(frankenterm-core): bound SemanticAnomalyWatchdog::drop to DROP_JOIN_TIMEOUT (ft-5vje2)

Follow-on from cod_4's ft-k3y0u Drop audit. Pre-fix, Drop did an
unbounded `JoinHandle::join()` on the ML thread; if the ML thread was
wedged inside a slow or hung `embed_fn` invocation it could not observe
`running=false`, and Drop would block its caller indefinitely. With
this fix, Drop always returns within `DROP_JOIN_TIMEOUT` (5s).

Implementation:

- New `pub(crate) const DROP_JOIN_TIMEOUT: Duration = 5s`. The Drop
  impl flips `running=false` (unchanged) then spawns a short-lived
  `ft-semantic-ml-join` helper that performs the actual `join()` and
  signals completion through a 1-slot `sync_channel`. The Drop body
  waits with `recv_timeout(DROP_JOIN_TIMEOUT)`. On timeout, Drop logs
  `tracing::warn!` and returns; the helper and the wedged ML thread
  finish on their own and the OS reclaims them at process exit. Failure
  to spawn the helper is logged at `error!` level and Drop still
  returns rather than blocking.
- The explicit `SemanticAnomalyWatchdog::shutdown` API (which still
  performs an unbounded join) remains the recommended path for
  deterministic teardown.

Tests (added to the existing `semantic_anomaly_watchdog::tests`
module):

- `watchdog_drop_is_bounded_when_embed_fn_hangs` — exercises the
  bounded fallback. Constructs a watchdog with an `embed_fn` that
  parks for `2 * DROP_JOIN_TIMEOUT`, submits a high-entropy segment so
  the ML thread enters `embed_fn`, then drops the watchdog and asserts
  Drop returned in `< DROP_JOIN_TIMEOUT + 2s` slack while still waiting
  at least `DROP_JOIN_TIMEOUT - 100ms` (so a future regression that
  short-circuits the wait can't make the regression-guard vacuous).
- `watchdog_drop_returns_promptly_when_thread_exits` — pins that the
  bound is a fallback, not a fixed delay. With a fast `mock_embed`
  and no submitted segments the idle ML thread exits on
  `running=false` within ms, and Drop returns in `< 1s`.

Verification (cargo test -p frankenterm-core --lib --no-default-features
'semantic_anomaly_watchdog::'): 28/28 pass, including the two new
bound tests. Lib build clean on `--no-default-features`. Pre-existing
feature-gated test breaks (`web_framework`, `wezterm_native`,
`fastembed`, `robot_envelope`, `web`, `toon_rust`) are unrelated and
predate this commit.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
